original: The life of an itinerant frontiersperson has its moments, but you also know it isn’t exactly sustainable. You’ve seen enough out here to know you’re extremely lucky to have your inheritance, and you absolutely intend to take up the reigns of the family business as Victor wanted. It’s not as if you can’t still have fun, is it? As best you can with the amount of scotch currently circulating your system, you put all of that to Ramis.

\n\n“Fair fucken points,” she replies slowly. “I guess I’ve never imagined having a goal like that in life - at least not once I paid off all of my debt.” She smiles at you. “I suppose it’s nice to have someone in charge who’s driven like that. We’re goen to get your dad’s silver, and no-one’s going to stand in your way, are they "+wY()+"? Here’s to you.”
\n\nShe gives you a lusty salute with her glass, and this time the amber fluid does slop everywhere.

You’ve known this quest was total nonsense, right from the start. What could surviving out here on the frontier possibly teach you about running a mega corporation like Steele Tech? Frankly, the only reason you’re doing it is to stop your rotten cousin from grabbing it - and to hang on to some of the most valuable assets Victor left you, like the incredible nursery and hangar on Tavros. Once you’ve succeeded, you fully intend to immediately step down as CEO, then carry on the exciting, fulfilling, sexy life you’ve found for yourself out here.
\n\nAs best you can with the amount of scotch currently circulating your system, you put all of that to Ramis.

\n\n“I knew it!” she cheers loudly. “You’re like ME "+wY()+", liven in the moment rather than dreaming of fucken boardrooms, ‘n money you won’t even know what to do with. You’ll keep me on, once you do get off the company leash, right? Oh, we’re goen to have so much fun!”
\n\nShe lustily bangs her glass into yours, and this time the amber fluid does slop everywhere.
We’ve had a few now captain, so tell me honestly.” Wag wag goes the glass, narrowly avoiding a spill. “Do you really want to be in charge of Steele Tech, once all this is over?”
Yes
No
You thank her for her time, and stand up.
You stumble slightly, the room seeming to tilt a bit. How much did you put away there?
\n\nRamis guffaws.

\n\n“No problem at all "+fY("cap’n","big lad","knickers")+"!” she grins. “You’re a good "+pc.mf("lad","girlee")+" for tryen to keep up with me whilst I’m layen out the Ramis family history. Don’t try’n fly the ship now!”
\n\nNo... no flying the ship. Maybe a lie down, now. You stumble back towards your quarters.
You have to steady yourself, blink a few times. That scotch really is too easy to drink.

\n\n“No probs, "+wY()+",” grins Ramis, shifting the bottle her way. “It’s ackshually quite good to talk about this stuff with you. Just umm...” she pours herself a fresh glass. “...needs a bit of fire to bring it out.”

textify(KY||(KY=(0,m.Z)([" Your pups are old enough to crawl, and they do it as much as they can. Sam could hardly be more delighted by this, and [sam.isPregnant |even in spite of her pregnancy ] she takes every opportunity to crawl around with them. As soon as you enter the room, the blonde ausar rushes over to the colorful, padded play area that’s been set up for your pups and drops to her knees, gleefully calling her children over to her with her tail wagging high. “Momma’s here, little ones! Come to Momma!” They all notice her immediately, and as they pick up her comforting scent, their tails mimic their mother’s, [var0] tufts of blonde fuzz swishing through the air as the precocious half-breed pups crawl towards the most important person in their world. They giggle and coo in excitement like any human child as they move, but some of their noises sound almost canine. That resemblance is only reinforced when they finally reach Sam and begin to wriggle energetically all over her. Your children are so excited that they barely even notice your presence, though whenever they do they yip and beam at you. The pups tug at their mother until she relents and collapses on her back in a giggling fit as they swarm all over her.[sam.isPregnant | She instinctively uses her tail to gently guide them away from the swell of her belly, preventing her overeager pups from possibly harming their unborn siblings. ] Sam’s giggling grows even louder as her children unintentionally tickle her with their wagging tails, and her laughter excites them even further. You settle on the floor next to this writhing mass of blonde fur and watch with amusement as the little ones vie for attention. Sam and your children are all practically screaming in delight after just a few moments like this, and the swamped ausar turns to you to beg for help, barely able to draw breath or speak between fits of laughter. “Ahahaha! [pc.name], please! Hahahaha! You’ve gottahahaha- you’ve gotta save me!!!” Such an earnest plea moves you to help immediately, and with an exaggerated growl you start grabbing pups at random, placing them on yourself and playfully roughhousing as much as is safe. Thanks to their ausar genetics, your offspring have developed faster than humans of their age, and this sort of minor horseplay is exactly what they love right now. Sam initially chides you, but when she sees how much the children enjoy it, she soon joins in, albeit more gently. The nursery is quickly filled with the sound of all your little pups squealing in delight. Sam sounds just as happy as they do, and even long after you start feeling worn down she plays with her pups tirelessly, filled with the boundless energy of a young mother. Her continued enthusiasm in the face of your waning stamina draws the children on you back to their mother. Luckily for her, they don’t have the staying power of an adult, and one by one the pups settle down until eventually they’re all asleep. The exertion seems to catch up with Sam all at once, and she practically crashes when the last pair of big blue eyes rolls shut. She pants softly under the pile of half-breeds, turning her head back towards you with a radiant smile. “Thank you,” she whispers, “for everything.” You smile back before leaning in to kiss her forehead. Then you climb to your [pc.feet] again as quietly as possible, fighting the temptation to just wrap your arms around your strange little family and fall asleep with them. textify(FY||(FY=(0,m.Z)([" You ask Sam to tell you about her past. [sam.trustRange 0 25 75 |“Okay,” she mutters flatly. The various ausar governments and companies have absolutely no idea what they’re doing in terms of cyber defenses. Maybe now that I have my own computer again I’ll reach out to him, since I don’t have to worry about our communications being traced.” Sam looks much more relaxed than usual as she talks about her father, obviously feeling nostalgic as she thinks over her fondest memories with a big smile. Where to begin though?” Sam rubs her forearm thoughtfully before choosing her topic. “Well, the scars on my face. They’re from my first boyfriend, both directly and indirectly. When I tried to leave him one night, he went to grab me by the hair. He missed though, and ended up clawing me really deeply.” You’re appalled at the thought of someone treating Sam so badly, much less actually injuring her. When Sam sees your reaction, she’s quick to elaborate, waving her blonde-furred hands disarmingly and stuttering in her haste to explain. “It’s not what you think! He didn’t scratch me on purpose, but it still scared the hell out of me, bad enough that I stayed that night instead of leaving. I was too afraid of him after that to even think about leaving. I never got the chance to find out if he ever would have actually gotten more physically abusive though. Just a few months after that, some rival pirates raided the base we were living on. My boyfriend and I got pinned in our room and cut off, and he used me as a distraction to try to run away.” Sam’s tone is one of anger now. “He got gunned down in the attempt, and I got slashed in the face by a big thraggen bitch’s sword since I couldn’t hold the door by myself. If he’d just stayed with me and helped me defend our room, he’d be alive, and I wouldn’t have these other scars. I mourned him at the time, but now that I’m older, I realize how horrible of a person he was. Good damn riddance!” Sam’s face is twisted in an angry snarl, surprising you a bit with her passion. “I’ve had other bad relationships, even a girl who pretended to love me so she could steal my code. I don’t hate any of them the same way I hate him though.” You pat Sam on the arm comfortingly and tell her that her past lovers didn’t deserve her. At that, her anger vanishes, replaced with a sorrowful look. “I’m not anything great that someone has to deserve,” she mutters quietly. The ausar bends her tail in front of herself and strokes it nervously for a moment as she gathers her thoughts. Finally, she starts explaining, “I was actually born in Gastigoth. My mom was heavily pregnant when she was arrested, and she gave birth shortly after arriving at the prison. It had been a difficult pregnancy for her, and I was her only pup. Although she hadn’t signed up for the breeding program, Gastigoth still does some basic genetic modification to all children born in the prison unless they’re a product of the breeding program and their free parent opts out of it. I didn’t really get all the usual enhancements, but they upped my intelligence and a few other things and removed the ‘potentially criminal’ traits from my genome, like violent tendencies.” You’re not even sure if you’re surprised to hear that or not. Involuntary prenatal modifications are extremely rare in most sectors, but it doesn’t sound so surprising for Gastigoth. Sam pauses for a moment to take a deep breath. This topic is apparently very emotional for her, but she soldiers on through her story, her gaze shifting down to the floor in an effort to hide her distress. “It wasn’t a free service. Gastigoth holds any babies they do this to until they’re an adult by the standards of their species if nobody pays for the mods, but my dad didn’t have enough money both to post bond for my mom and to pay for my gene mods. My dad really loved my mom, but she said she’d never forgive him if he left me to grow up in jail, so he chose me instead. Before my dad could save up more money to buy my mom out, some rich asshole swooped in and bought her. We’ve never seen her since. Gods only know what awful things may have happened to her.” Sam seems on the verge of crying as she continues her story. “I’ve always blamed myself for my mom getting sold like that. If I hadn’t been born, my dad wouldn’t have had to waste all of his money on me and he could’ve saved her. I can’t even be proud of my talents without feeling guilty. I just feel so horrible for separating them like that, like I’m a burden for my dad that just reminds him what he could’ve had instead.” Sam sniffles pitifully as hot tears begin to stream down her scarred face. “I’m a horrible daughter, a cowardly pirate, and a terrible person. I don’t want to hurt people physically, so I couldn’t even be a proper Black Void member, but I’ve hurt so many people in so many other ways with all of my hacking. I’ve ruined hundreds of lives, maybe thousands, including my own parents’. I don’t know how I can ever make up for all this.” The ausar puts her face in her hands and sobs quietly. textify(JY||(JY=(0,m.Z)([" You’ve known Sam long enough now that you’re confident she’ll open up to you on a topic that has left you more than a little confused.
